description_long: This guard stone features a decorative dolphin sculpture leaping from stylized waves, all rendered in a dark, stone-like material. The backdrop is a semi-circular, scalloped wall made of lighter stone, giving the impression of a shell or fountain niche. The base is a rounded, shallow basin, also in a dark stone, with a raised lip at the front. The dolphin is centrally positioned and appears to be the focal point, with its body arched and fins extended. The overall construction is robust and ornamental, suggesting both a protective and aesthetic function. The materials and design evoke a sense of permanence and classical urban decor, suitable for public spaces.
description: A decorative stone guard stone with a dolphin sculpture leaping from waves, set against a scalloped, shell-like stone backdrop and a rounded basin base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dolphin sculpture, scalloped stone backdrop, and the rounded basin with raised lip.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the dolphin and the depth of the basin and backdrop.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid, scalloped stone wall with no visible sculpture details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the dolphin's other profile and the curvature of the basin and backdrop.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to parks, plazas, or along pedestrian walkways in urban environments, often serving as a decorative barrier or protective marker near public gathering spaces.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, upright, with the base resting flush on a paved or stone surface.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show smoothing and slight discoloration on the basin lip and dolphin sculpture due to occasional contact and weathering; possible minor chipping at the base from incidental impacts; some erosion or patina on the upper surfaces from rain and pollution.
